text.title: Autofix-vs-Flag Decision Rule for Coding Agents Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules pinning the methodology's preconditions, output shape, and skip path. Each rule pairs a binary check with rationale + named source.

rule.statement: An agent applies an autofix automatically IFF the fix is purely syntactic (formatter, import sort, type annotation insertion, dead-code removal). Semantic fixes are flag-only.
rule.rationale: Syntactic fixes are reversible; semantic fixes can alter behaviour.

rule.statement: The tool MUST ship an explicit --fix / --write / --autofix flag with a documented fix: block. Heuristic 'maybe applies' fixes are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Tools without explicit fix authority guess; guessed fixes break code.

rule.statement: After autofix, the relevant test slice MUST be run; rollback on failure.
rule.rationale: Without behavioural verification, autofix can silently regress.

rule.statement: Autofix proceeds IFF resulting diff is < 50 lines OR every change matches a single rule ID.
rule.rationale: Large multi-rule diffs become refactors that humans should review.

rule.statement: Every autofix-vs-flag decision MUST emit a JSONL record (tool, rule_id, decision, diff_size, tests_passed). Append-only.
rule.rationale: Audit log lets the team see the agent's actual judgement; tuning happens against data.

rule.statement: If no tests exist OR tool autofix known-buggy OR diff > 50 lines AND > 1 rule ID, STOP and flag-only.
rule.rationale: Skip is the safe default when any condition fails.
